title: Pecluma dispersa
taxonomy: Pecluma dispersa is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Pecluma, which is a group of epiphytic ferns native to Central and South America. Pecluma dispersa is a delicate, air-purifying plant with slender fronds that thrive in shaded environments. Its classification is further divided into the family Polypodiaceae, order Polypodiales, class Polypodiopsida, and division Pteridophyta. This species is often cultivated for its attractive foliage and ability to grow in a wide range of conditions.

intro: Pecluma dispersa, also known as Bird's Nest Fern or Elkhorn Fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain, inflammation, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Pecluma dispersa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pecluma dispersa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues.
